# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 106.934 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.7370
  biquad b0=1.00044331786 b1=-1.99780184549 b2=0.997407457823 a1=-1.99780184549 a2=0.997850775684
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 89.722 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.0700
  biquad b0=1.00049174666 b1=-1.99758154839 b2=0.997124244102 a1=-1.99758154839 a2=0.997615990761
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 46.509 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.4878
  biquad b0=1.00035476951 b1=-1.99827080402 b2=0.99792529243 a1=-1.99827080402 a2=0.998280061942
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 15.015 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.2386
  biquad b0=1.00013765069 b1=-1.99933169805 b2=0.99919501274 a1=-1.99933169805 a2=0.999332663434
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 71.045 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.7422
  biquad b0=1.00021553582 b1=-1.99893346293 b2=0.99873953714 a1=-1.99893346293 a2=0.998955072958
